fixture.source_domain: Insert, replace, update, delete, field formats, and invalid text guards

cases.focus: INSERT SQL shape, explicit formats, NULL value omission from prepare values, affected rows, and insert_id mutation
field_types-driven format selection for REPLACE plus replace insert_id/rows_affected state
SET NULL and WHERE IS NULL SQL shape with mixed data/where formats
DELETE WHERE value formatting and null comparison handling
invalid helper type rejection and insert_id reset boundary
public write method array guards before field processing
format pairing, numeric charset/length skips, string charset metadata, and null passthrough
strip_invalid_text truncation detection, plural last_error text, and failed field processing
explicit format cycling and field_types fallback mapping
direct truncation/pass-through output without process_fields rejection

native_boundaries.reason: The probe overrides wpdb::query() with deterministic write return values so insert/replace/update/delete SQL construction and state mutation can be compared without a live mysqli connection.
The write methods still call WordPress wpdb::prepare(); low-level _real_escape() is made deterministic here because dedicated prepare/escaping parity is already recorded by WPHX-305.02.

get_col_charset() and get_col_length() normally depend on live table metadata. The fixture supplies deterministic charset/length metadata and WP_Error cases to exercise process_field_charsets(), process_field_lengths(), and strip_invalid_text().
Protected process-field helpers are exposed only through the fixture subclass so their native PHP array mutation and comparison behavior can be recorded before Haxe ownership.

WordPress database error: Processing the values for the following fields failed: short_text, byte_text. The supplied values may be too long or contain invalid data.

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side is a copied WordPress oracle source tree until wpdb write/process-field helpers are promoted behind typed Haxe parity candidates.
This fixture avoids real MySQL. Live affected rows, insert IDs, duplicate-key replace behavior, and mysqli write errors need the database runtime integration harness.
strip_invalid_text() cases stay in local ASCII/latin1 truncation paths. The branch that asks MySQL to convert invalid text remains a later charset/collation plus DB runtime gate.
This fixture covers seed traces from the upstream db tests. Full upstream wpdb PHPUnit parity remains a domain-level closure requirement.
